node.html:
Built a real estate price prediction tool powered by an XGBoost model trained on Canadian housing data.
Users can input property details like city, province, furnishing, and availability — and instantly get predicted rent.
Coordinates are auto-filled from the address using OpenStreetMap APIs for geo-features.

frontmatter.title: Indian Sign Language Recognition using MediaPipe and LSTM

node.html:
Developed a deep learning-based sign language recognition system using LSTM models and MediaPipe's pose/hand tracking to identify Indian Sign Language (ISL) gestures from video inputs.

Key Highlights

  • Processed video frames using MediaPipe to extract keypoints (hands, pose, and face landmarks).
  • Built a robust LSTM model trained on multiple gesture classes for real-time recognition.
  • Deployed interactive demo using Streamlit for users to test with video uploads.
  • Achieved over 95% accuracy across validation sets with real-world ISL samples.

Developed crowd density-based decision-making algorithm for optimizing smart room devices, with a focus on minimizing power consumption. Utilized RSSI (Received Signal Strength Indicator) input from smart room setups to estimate crowd density, offering a cost-efficient alternative to video-based solutions.

frontmatter.title: Twitter Bot Detection with Textual and Numeric Feature Set

node.html: The project aims to detect Twitter bots to prevent the spread of false information using machine learning algorithms. By analyzing both textual and numeric features, the system labels users as genuine or fake. The project compares various algorithms like KNN, SVM, and Adaboost to determine the most effective approach for bot detection.
